schema_version.description: SchemaVer (MODEL-REVISION-ADDITION). NOT SemVer. 1-0-1 adds the optional `relation` body (epistemic core); 1-0-0 records (provenance-only) remain valid — additive, append-only ledger never rewritten.

conformsTo.description: Per-record schema URI for export-compat checks (RO-Crate compatible). v1.0.1 = +relation body.

annotation_id.description: ann_ + first 16 hex chars of sha256_hex(canonical_json(stable_tuple)).

source_id.description: Canonical corpus source_id (e.g. doi_10_1097_fpc_..., pmid_12345, sha_abcdef...).

id.description: Stable agent identifier per urn:agent:<type>:<name>[@<version>] convention.

instrument.description: RO-Crate SoftwareApplication used by the agent (the tool, not the actor).

scope.description: Free-form scope tag (e.g. raw_fetch, parse, claim_extraction, verdict, annotation).

tool.description: Optional free-form tool name (callsite-defined; informational, not for identity).

result.description: Output reference (flat namespace per OpenTelemetry GenAI). Sidecar for >4KB outputs.

output_uri.description: Direct URI to a sidecar output (corpus:// or project-root:// or repo://).

source_content_hash.description: Hash of the source bytes the annotation was made against (freshness gate).

asserted_at.description: When the agent asserted this (world time). UTC ISO-8601 with Z suffix.

endTime.description: Alias for asserted_at — RO-Crate export compatibility.

recorded_at.description: Corpus-writer-assigned append time (transaction time). UTC ISO-8601.

idempotency_key.description: Canonical-JSON of the stable tuple. Used to derive annotation_id.

valid_from.description: Phase A: world-time the assertion becomes valid (bitemporal). Informational; defaults to asserted_at when the writer omits. NOT part of annotation_stable_tuple — adding it does not mutate annotation_id.

canonicalize_version.description: Phase F sidecar: which canonical_json form was used to derive annotation_id. Absent (= legacy) for all historical records; v1 only on new records that opted into NFC + pinned-timestamp canonicalization. NOT part of annotation_stable_tuple.

relation.description: Epistemic core (schema 1-0-1): a grounded claim relation carried inline on a scope='claim_relation' annotation. Self-contained (spans referenced by id, not full text) so the record stays small and the append stays atomic — no sidecar. Projected into claim_relations + claim_relation_endpoints by corpus_core.index.

relation_id.description: rel_ + first 16 hex of sha256(canonical_json({relation_class, sorted(subject_refs), sorted(object_refs), detector})).

relation_class.description: ClaimFlow 5-class. STORED in full (deep representation); inference operates on refute/support (operate-simple).

kind.description: Home-repo taxonomy when present (e.g. phenome contradiction kind: temporal/magnitude/mechanism/subject_scope).

subject_refs.description: Namespaced participant IDs on the subject side (multi-party): corpus:<source_id> / repo:<repo>:<kind>:<id> / local:<id> / internal:<repo>.

object_refs.description: Namespaced participant IDs on the object side (multi-party).

spans.description: Grounded span references (id + location, not full text — text lives in the home repo/parse).

grade_weight.description: GRADE-style evidence weight in [0,1] (nullable). Feeds the linear support_balance view; never a probability.

detector.description: Origin of the adjudication: repo+tool+prompt_hash (e.g. phenome:contradiction-detect:<sha8>). Part of relation_id identity.

home_pair_id.description: Round-trip key to the home contradiction_pairs.pair_id (phenome) when applicable.

home_verdict_id.description: Round-trip key to the home verdict id (genomics) when applicable.
